items: What are you thankful for and what has been challenging for you since we last met?
Have you experienced God at work in your life lately?
Have you had the opportunity to care for your friends or neighbours this week?
How did you go with your application from the last time we met?
Read the passage aloud and then watch the video clip.
Have one person retell the passage in their own words with the group’s help.
Was there anything missed or added?

data.html:
9To some who were confident of their own righteousness and looked down on everyone else, Jesus told this parable:10“Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.11The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other people—robbers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.12I fast twice a week and give a tenth of all I get.’

13“But the tax collector stood at a distance. He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, ‘God, have mercy on me, a sinner.’

14“I tell you that this man, rather than the other, went home justified before God. For all those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.”

items: What stands out for you in this passage?
What does this passage tell you about Jesus, God, people and you?
What does this passage say about being good enough?
If this passage is from God, what difference would it make in your life?
How are you going to apply this passage this week?
Who will you share this passage with?
